title: Run in morning — snap map app concept and pre-college todo
html:
TL;DR

  • July 22, 2019 morning todo: "start concept for snap map app", "read thy books", "look at the gaming laptop", "make money", "write shit down", "college situation" — six-line scaffolding of pre-senior-year Yahir's parallel pursuits.
  • "Snap map app" — earliest documented product idea in the vault, surfacing two months before the Stanford via business pep talk frames startup-building as a college admissions lever.
  • "Read thy books" in the same line as "make money" and "college situation" — the self improvement as stack frame already operational at 17.
  • "Look at the gaming laptop" — recurs days later in 2019 07 24 decide on best gaming laptop; the laptop search was an active 2019 thread.
  • "Write shit down" recursively endorses the habit inside the junk drawer — proto-meta-note.

Key quote

"start concept for snap map app"

Why it matters in the graph

Six lines, six parallel tracks — fitness (run), books, hardware, money, writing, college. This is the earliest fully-articulated stacked pursuit morning-note in the 2019 corpus. The format — verb-led bullets with no elaboration — is the form crystallizing.

The snap map app is a single line, but it's the first build idea logged in the same breath as "college situation" — the early seed of the build to get into Stanford thesis that Yahir would briefly chase. No follow-up surfaced; the idea died in the note.

Wikilinks

snap map app · snapchat · 10x thinking · 2019 07 24 decide on best gaming laptop · 2019 09 26 youre only chance of getting into stanford is to start a bus · bill gates
